case_name: Medstar Georgetown Medical Center, Inc. v. Kaplan

summary.brief: Medstar Georgetown Medical Center, Inc. v. Kaplan. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: A jury found MedStar Georgetown Medical Center, Inc. and MMG-GI at Lafayette Center d/b/a MedStar Medical Group II, LLC liable for breaching the national standard of care when treating David Kaplan
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: awarded him an aggregate of $4 million in damages
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the judgment of the Superior Court.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Mr. Kaplan presented evidence at trial that he had suffered two distinct kinds of harm. He experienced physical injury: the continuation of his severe Crohn’s disease that did not respond to the steroids MedStar prescribed, the complete deterioration of his hip bones, and the eventual replacement of both hips, which involved three hip surgeries with lengthy and painful recoveries.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: And he experienced emotional distress. Previously an “intense runn[er],” as a result of his hip replacements he suffered the loss of participating in an activity that he loved and dating as a result of his hip replacements, particularly since “intimate activities” were “impacted.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court correctly noted that “[t]he jury is afforded great weight in determining the appropriate measure of damages” and explained that a jury’s verdict should only be overturned by the court if it “resulted from passion, prejudice, mistake, oversight, or consideration of improper elements or [is] beyond all reason or . . . so great as to shock the conscience.”
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Severe Crohn's disease symptoms and hip pain
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Continuation of severe Crohn's disease not responding to steroids, deterioration of hip bones
medications: Steroids (specific type and dosage not documented in judgment)
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Severe Crohn's disease with hip deterioration

requestables.name: Gastroenterology consult
requestables.description: Consultation for management of Crohn's disease

reveal.result_summary: Continuation of severe Crohn's disease not responding to steroids

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with severe Crohn's disease symptoms

key_events.event: Patient reports severe symptoms and hip pain

timeline_phases.name: Workup and Management
timeline_phases.description: Assessment and management of Crohn's disease

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with severe Crohn's disease on prolonged steroid therapy
decision_points.prompt: What is the appropriate management for steroid-refractory Crohn's disease?

options.clinical_reasoning: Biologics are indicated for steroid-refractory Crohn's disease

options.description: Continue steroid therapy beyond recommended duration
options.clinical_reasoning: Prolonged steroid use is not recommended due to side effects

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not transition to biologics

expected_action_court.description: Biologic therapy should have been initiated
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: National guidelines recommend biologics for steroid-refractory cases

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Prolonged steroid use exceeded safe duration
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Biologics should have been initiated within three months
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Breach of national standard of care

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent three hip surgeries with lengthy and painful recoveries
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of ability to run and participate in physical activities

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to transition to biologics
Causation established - prolonged steroid use led to hip deterioration

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Biologics would have controlled Crohn's disease without prolonged steroid use

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents with severe Crohn's disease symptoms

court_findings.legal_significance: Breach of national standard of care
